questions.text: Two triangles are congruent if their corresponding sides and angles are:
questions.explanation: Congruent triangles have exactly equal corresponding sides and angles.

questions.text: The SAS congruence rule states that two triangles are congruent if:

options.a: two sides and the included angle are equal
options.b: two angles and one side are equal

questions.explanation: SAS: two Sides and the included Angle (angle between those sides) must be equal.

questions.text: The SSS congruence rule states that two triangles are congruent when:

options.d: two angles and a side are equal

questions.explanation: SSS: all three pairs of corresponding Sides must be equal.

questions.text: In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite the equal sides are:
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, base angles (angles opposite equal sides) are always equal.

questions.text: The RHS congruence rule applies only to:
questions.explanation: RHS (Right angle-Hypotenuse-Side) is a special rule that applies only to right-angled triangles.

questions.text: The ASA congruence rule states that two triangles are congruent if:

options.a: two sides and included angle are equal
options.b: two angles and the included side are equal

questions.explanation: ASA: two Angles and the Side between them (included side) must be equal.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. Then which angles are equal?
questions.explanation: AB = AC means the triangle is isosceles. Angle B (opposite AC) and angle C (opposite AB) are the base angles and are equal.

questions.text: If two sides of a triangle are unequal, the angle opposite the longer side is:
questions.explanation: In a triangle, the greater angle is opposite the longer side.

questions.text: The sum of any two sides of a triangle is always:
questions.explanation: Triangle inequality: the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the third side.

questions.text: In an equilateral triangle, each angle measures:
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has all angles equal: 180 / 3 = 60 degrees each.

questions.text: The AAS congruence rule states two triangles are congruent if:

options.a: two angles and a non-included side are equal
options.b: two sides and one angle are equal
options.d: two angles and the included side are equal

questions.explanation: AAS: two Angles and a non-included Side (not between those angles) must be equal.

questions.text: The longest side of a right-angled triangle is called the:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is the side opposite the right angle and is always the longest side.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if angle A > angle B, then:
questions.explanation: The side opposite the greater angle is longer. Angle A is opposite BC and angle B is opposite AC. If angle A > angle B, then BC > AC.

questions.text: Congruent triangles always have:
questions.explanation: Congruent figures are identical in both shape and size.

questions.text: In right triangle ABC right-angled at C, the hypotenuse is:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is always opposite the right angle. Right angle is at C, so AB is the hypotenuse.

questions.text: If triangle ABC is congruent to triangle DEF (written ABC ≅ DEF), then AB corresponds to:
questions.explanation: In the notation ABC ≅ DEF, the vertices correspond in order: A to D, B to E, C to F. So AB corresponds to DE.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle is also:
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has all sides equal, so it satisfies the definition of isosceles (at least two sides equal).

questions.text: Which of the following can be sides of a triangle?
questions.explanation: For 3,4,5: 3+4=7>5 ✓, 3+5=8>4 ✓, 4+5=9>3 ✓. Option a: 1+2=3, not greater than 3. Options b and d also fail the triangle inequality.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, the side opposite to angle B is:
questions.explanation: The side opposite to angle B is the side that does not touch vertex B, which is AC.

questions.text: If the bisector of angle A of triangle ABC is perpendicular to BC, the triangle is:
questions.explanation: If the angle bisector from A is perpendicular to BC, then AB = AC, making the triangle isosceles.

questions.text: In isosceles triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle A = 40 degrees. Find angle B:
questions.explanation: angle B = angle C = (180 - 40) / 2 = 140 / 2 = 70 degrees.

questions.text: In triangles ABC and DEF, AB = DE, BC = EF and angle B = angle E. By which rule are they congruent?
questions.explanation: Two sides (AB=DE, BC=EF) and the included angle (angle B = angle E between those sides). This is SAS.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm and AC = 9 cm. The largest angle is opposite which side?
questions.explanation: The largest angle is opposite the longest side. AC = 9 cm is the longest side.

questions.text: In right triangle ABC right-angled at B, AB = 3 cm and BC = 4 cm. Find AC (hypotenuse):
In triangle ABC, the exterior angle at C is 120 degrees. If angle A = angle B, find angle A:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = angle A + angle B = 2 angle A = 120 degrees, so angle A = 60 degrees.

questions.text: In triangles PQR and XYZ, angle P = angle X, angle Q = angle Y and PR = XZ. By which rule are they congruent?
questions.explanation: Two angles equal (P=X, Q=Y) and side PR = XZ. PR is not between angles P and Q, so this is AAS.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. The bisector of angle A meets BC at D. Which of the following is true?

options.d: AD is not perpendicular to BC

questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the angle bisector from the apex bisects the base, so BD = DC.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle B = 50 degrees and angle C = 60 degrees. Which is the longest side?
questions.explanation: angle A = 180 - 50 - 60 = 70 degrees. Largest angle is A, so longest side is BC (opposite angle A).

questions.text: In right-angled triangles ABC and DEF, angle B = angle E = 90 degrees, hypotenuse AC = DF and BC = EF. Which rule proves congruence?
questions.explanation: Right angle, Hypotenuse equal, and one Side equal: this is the RHS congruence rule.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC = 5 cm. If angle B = 55 degrees, find angle A:
questions.explanation: Since AB = AC, angle B = angle C = 55 degrees. angle A = 180 - 55 - 55 = 70 degrees.

questions.text: In isosceles triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle ABC = 70 degrees. The bisector of angle B meets AC at D. Find angle BDC:
questions.explanation: angle BAC = 180 - 70 - 70 = 40 degrees. angle ABD = 35 degrees (bisector). In triangle ABD: angle ADB = 180 - 40 - 35 = 105 degrees. angle BDC = 180 - 105 = 75 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = 4 cm and AC = 5 cm. Which of the following is a valid range for BC?
questions.explanation: Triangle inequality: |AC - AB| < BC < AC + AB, so |5-4| < BC < 5+4, giving 1 cm < BC < 9 cm.

questions.text: In triangles ABC and PQR, angle A = angle P, angle B = angle Q and AB = PQ. Which rule proves congruence?
questions.explanation: Two angles (A=P, B=Q) and the included side AB = PQ (side between angle A and angle B). This is ASA.

questions.text: In isosceles triangle ABC, AB = AC. D is the midpoint of BC. Which of the following is true about AD?

options.b: AD is perpendicular to BC only
options.c: AD bisects angle A and is perpendicular to BC

questions.explanation: In isosceles triangle with AB=AC, the median from apex A to base BC also bisects angle A and is perpendicular to BC.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle A = 90 degrees and AB = AC. Find angle B:
questions.explanation: AB = AC means angle B = angle C (angles opposite equal sides). angle B + angle C = 90 (since angle A = 90). So 2 angle B = 90, angle B = 45 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, PQ = QR and angle Q = 80 degrees. S is the midpoint of PR. Find angle QSR:
questions.explanation: PQ = QR means triangle PQR is isosceles. QS is the median from Q to PR, so QS is also the perpendicular bisector of PR. Thus angle QSR = 90 degrees.

questions.text: In isosceles triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle A = 50 degrees. Find angle B (in degrees).
questions.explanation: angle B = angle C = (180 - 50) / 2 = 65 degrees.

questions.text: In right triangle ABC right-angled at C, AB = 13 cm and BC = 5 cm. Find AC (in cm).
An exterior angle of a triangle is 135 degrees and one non-adjacent interior angle is 75 degrees. Find the other non-adjacent interior angle (in degrees).
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of non-adjacent interior angles: 135 = 75 + x, x = 60 degrees.

questions.text: In equilateral triangle ABC, find each angle (in degrees).
questions.explanation: All angles of an equilateral triangle are equal: 180 / 3 = 60 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. If angle BAC = 100 degrees, find angle ABC (in degrees).
questions.explanation: angle ABC = angle ACB = (180 - 100) / 2 = 40 degrees.

questions.text: Can a triangle have sides of length 3 cm, 4 cm and 8 cm? Write 1 for Yes, 0 for No.
questions.explanation: 3 + 4 = 7, which is less than 8. This violates the triangle inequality, so no triangle is possible.

questions.text: Triangles ABC and DEF are congruent. AB = 4 cm, BC = 5 cm, CA = 6 cm. If AB corresponds to DE, find QR. (Here QR means EF, the side corresponding to BC.)
questions.explanation: BC corresponds to EF. Since the triangles are congruent, EF = BC = 5 cm.

questions.text: In triangles ABC and DEF, AB = DE = 3 cm, angle A = angle D = 70 degrees and AC = DF = 5 cm. Name the congruence rule used (write SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS or RHS).
questions.explanation: Two sides (AB=DE, AC=DF) and the included angle (angle A = angle D between those sides). This is SAS.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle A : angle B : angle C = 1:2:3. Find the largest angle (in degrees).
questions.explanation: x + 2x + 3x = 180, 6x = 180, x = 30. Largest angle = 3x = 90 degrees.

questions.text: In isosceles triangle ABC, AB = AC = 5 m and BC = 8 m. D is the midpoint of BC. Find AD (in metres).
questions.explanation: D is midpoint of BC, so BD = 4 m. AD is perpendicular to BC (property of isosceles triangle). AD^2 = AB^2 - BD^2 = 25 - 16 = 9, so AD = 3 m.

cases.case_text: Two students Riya and Siya are making kite frames for a school competition. Riya's kite frame is made of two triangles ABC and ADC sharing the common side AC. The measurements are AB = AD = 30 cm and BC = DC = 20 cm. They need to verify that both halves of the kite are identical so the kite flies symmetrically.

subparts.text: In triangles ABC and ADC, which congruence rule proves they are congruent?
subparts.explanation: AB=AD, BC=DC and AC=AC (common side). All three sides are equal, so SSS applies.

subparts.text: Since triangles ABC and ADC are congruent, angle BAC = angle DAC. This means diagonal AC:
subparts.explanation: Since angle BAC = angle DAC, AC divides angle A into two equal parts, so AC bisects angle A.

subparts.text: If angle BAD = 80 degrees and AC bisects angle BAD, find angle BAC (in degrees).

cases.case_text: A builder is constructing a roof in the shape of isosceles triangle ABC where AB = AC = 5 m and BC = 8 m. The apex A represents the top of the roof. To reinforce the structure, the builder erects a vertical support AD from A perpendicular to the base BC, where D is the midpoint of BC. The builder needs to find the height of this support for material estimation.

subparts.text: Since triangle ABC is isosceles with AB = AC, which angles are equal?
subparts.explanation: In isosceles triangle with AB = AC, the base angles angle B and angle C are equal.

subparts.text: AD divides triangle ABC into two smaller triangles ABD and ACD. Which rule proves triangles ABD and ACD are congruent?
subparts.explanation: AB=AC, BD=DC (D is midpoint of BC), AD=AD (common). All three sides equal: SSS.

subparts.text: Find the length of support AD using Pythagorean theorem. (AB = 5 m, BD = 4 m since D is midpoint of BC = 8 m). Give answer in metres.
